reviews.review_description:
Let me start this review by giving you a fair warning: Dreadhalls is old. Like, ancient for VR standards. Its first version dates back to 2013 i believe. While this is an "updated" version, it still is pretty dated for todays VR standards. So if you want a advanced vr game with valve level interaction, this isnt the game. You have been warned.
Now to the actual Review:



Thousands of horror titles on the Quests, and somehow, the one who came semi first is still the scariest, and not for the faint of heart.

Dreadhalls, man where do i start? Do i start at the fact the game gives you a feeling of dread the first second of opening the game up? Or the (albeit "low quality" looking) charming art style, looking like a slender game? Or the down right beautiful soundtrack? Theres a lot to tell, so ill try my best.

Dreadhalls, from second 1, sends you into a claustrophobic dungeon complex with no memory if you are or what you once were, with a oil lamp that has to be constantly refilled, so you dont lose your only light source. No explanation, no "this is who you are and what you are doing here". even if that is the case, there is a story being told through scrolls though, you just have to read them. A good balance between no explanation and lore being hidden in the dreaded halls. Theres also the "stone faces" who can aswell give you snippets of lore about this dungeon, and a bit about yourself and why you are here. The catch? Not only do you have to pay with coins for answers, and you can only ask one question, they also tell it to you in sort of riddles. The world feels tight, like its trying to strangle you, and all the maps (except the tutorial) are all randomly generated, offering immense amounts of replayability. The "story" mode introduces the stars of the show, the monsters, one by one. If thats too boring for you, you can also pick the "random map" mode, that flings you into a Level, with all monsters at once, again, very high levels of replayability here. Both game modes objective is to find eyes hidden somewhere in the dungeon.

The game doesnt have "jump scares", atleast in the sense of scripted scares. The game creates, due to the randomly genersted maps, a very natrual Environment for scares, that will make even the slightest scares ten times scarier. The atmosphere is unmatched, and immersion, even with its dated features, is at its max. Let yourself get immersed, and youll be crapping your pants in no time.

The soundtrack, is suprisingly high quality and well done for a VR game, even for today. It just sounds neat. While the game uses its soundtrack only occasionally, during certain moments, it shines in the fact it doesnt have constant music to accompany you. It makes you feel alone, and isolated.


The monsters also have a nice varity of different Features. You got a weeping angel like statue, a witch you cant look at or she will chase you, a massive meatball that detects movement and so on. They work together really well, creating natrually terrifying moments when encountering multiple monsters at once.

So, is dreadhalls scary? Yes. Yes absolutely. This is one of the only games that managed to get me in active fear, to the point i needed to take breaks. Maybe im just a scaredy cat, but that is my, and several others, experience, even those who are not scaredy cats, are terrifyed of playing this title. 

Now, i see a lot of people wanting multiplayer, but personally this is one of those games that should stay singleplayer. Multiplayer would reduce it in scariness in my opinion.


Overall, Dreadhalls is a game that is well worth the price of admission, and even with it being an oldie, it is probably the scariest game youll be playing, ever.

10/10, will crap my pants again.
I shared this game at a party, 3 people didn’t dare to leave the starting room 😂
Pretty self explainetory from the title
Epic
Would be much better as multi-player. I love them game but I am too scared to play by myself. Would be 5 stars if was multi-player.

I read about this being basic but frightening. That's about right. The one thing they got right was the name as the feeling of dread is constant. Played about an hour so far and had a couple of brown pants moments. The graphics were not as bad as I imagined, I do have the optimiser on though so unsure if it's different without it. It's not for the faint hearted that's for sure. It's pretty immersive and if you want a good scare, they don't come much better, or should I say worse, than this.
This is one of the best horror VR games I've played this for a while now off and on but it's so scary and enjoyable!
Bought it in sale, really enjoying it and wondering why I never bought it ages ago   Dead creepy

Best horror game i have played by far. Definitely made me jump numerous times even after knowing what to expect. After seeing this game on youtube and playing a similar free to play game on my phone called Dungeon Nightmares i have always wanted to play this game. I am glad i got to experience this game. Its a must have game. Worth the price. Its a bit short but you can play on randomized maps for replayability so thats a big plus. Graphics are also not too bad and pretty smooth looking. Great job devs. Hope to see another scary game from you guys.

reviews.review_title: Starts off to easy. ends to hard
reviews.review_description: Don’t get me wrong the game ￼is absolutely spectacular! It scared me so hard I scream at the most highest pitch scream￼ breaking everything glass ￼but, the difficulty curve is way way too short and deep. ￼ like I wish there’s a sound queue￼￼￼￼￼ to tell if something is close. Because it’s way too hard to tell something’s there. If you can’t run or hide most of the time because your stamina sucks so you can’t run away from the rat and Mr. Spike￼￼￼￼￼￼ guy. (sorry if my points are wrong.)
A simple, yet effective, horror game. The atmosphere, the emptiness, the demonic whispering, etc. Guaranteed chills when I launch the game. Highly recommended.
Dreadhalls is a fckn scary game for me i get paranoid right when i take the headset off but what I do want it to have is a couple more monsters and dif maps please if u could devs

The first and still the best horror game available on the quest, If I last 10mins before ripping of my headset it's a miracle. Great atmosphere and sound, truly terrifying. Heart jumps out your chest everytime you turn a corner. Would love to see a quest 3 graphic update. If you want to show your friends VR for the first time and witness them scream like a baby, this is the game to show them.

reviews.review_title: Step back in time to the 90s
reviews.review_description:
After reading a bunch of reviews claiming that this game is almost to terrifying to play, I was really excited to give it a try. It only took about five minutes to be completely disappointed.

Not sure what all the hype is about. Playing this game is like regressing back to the early 90s, technologically. The graphics are laughable compared to today's standards, and so is the gameplay. You basically just wander around in a dungeon maze where each hall looks just like the last, while pointing and clicking to pick up objects. Maybe there's more to it at some point later in the game, but from what I've seen so far, I highly doubt it would get much better.

And, as far as being scary, just LOL.

This was the first horror game I played in VR and, having played other ones, I can say that this is the scariest. For whatever reason, I find this game to be the most terrifying and experience, with just the right balance of suspense, ambience, and of course dread. There aren’t cheap jump, scares so much as things that emerge out of the darkness in ways the feel less like a horror film and more like actually exploring a dark labyrinth. 

I wish they would make a sequel !

I don't know how they managed to make a game this scary; to the point where it traumatized Markiplier, but after only playing for an hour, yeah, I can safely say that I was extremely terrified. Something about the claustrophobic environment and the close proximity of the monsters, the atmosphere and the music make this game completely terrifying. It's dark, it's damp, it's miserable, you will be forced to hide on multiple occasions. You will solve puzzles, strange women on the walls will watch you until you talk to them, and yes, their gaze will follow you! 

Dreadhalls made me scared to put on my Quest 3. I dunno how you guys did that, but congratulations.

OK, so I saw Dreadhalls announced a few weeks/months ago and being a big fan of horror games it piqued my interest. So I read a few reviews of the game I thought "Really!? How can all these people be freaked out and scared of this game?"
So, yesterday I saw it was only £7.99 on the store and decided to buy it. On goes the headset and off I go on my journey down the dark, damp corridors.

Now let me just start off by saying, I don't scare easily. I've played all the Resident Evil games, all the Silent Hill games plus P. T, and horror movies just don't do anything for me.
I've been praying for something to scare the absolute sh** out of me for years and now, all of a sudden, I'm walking into the darkness of a dungeon with the hairs on my neck standing right up, my butt is clenched so tight it could crack a walnut. I've not felt like this since my first trip to Silent Hill!

I started exploring the dark corridors, peeking around the doors, sneaking slowly into small rooms filled with skeletons. I'm all too aware that something is stalking me, but I can't see it, I can only hear it. The audio in this game is truly spectacular. 

Then it happens! I walk into a room with a terrible looking witch/ghost woman and just stand there staring at her back. It slowly turns around whilst hissing and screaming at me as I felt myself become frozen to the spot. Then, without warning, it just starts chasing, the music ramps up in my headphones, which is almost as startling as the ghost! I start running away in horror, until I get a good distance away. Phew! I start to relax a little, "she's not so fast" I thought to myself... Then it dawned on me, there was a horrible noise coming from behind me, it sounded rabid barking/growling? At this point I'm honestly too scared to look. My sprinting victory from the ghost no longer mattered, for as I slowly and reluctantly turned around, I was greeted by this huge hulking beast dog, which lunged right into my face killing me instantly. I let out a scream which also frightened my wife, who was sitting in the same room, oblivious to my demise.

So, I took off the headset, my palms sweating, my body shaking...

Would I do it again? Definitely! This game is what I've been waiting for and although I'm not a religious person, I felt the urge to pray to jesus.

9/10 from me.
I have still not finished did. But can recommend. Just don't look at them 😭

This game is very old, and it doesn’t matter.  The concept is solid, the controls are perfect and the graphics get the job done.  I still like jumping back in and screaming like a little girl in my living room when I stumble onto one of the monsters.  If you like horror games, this one is a must have.

reviews.review_title: I LOVE  horror movies and games, but
reviews.review_description: this particular game... Holy crap it's frightening. It's difficult to tell from the preview video but when you are actually in the game the visuals, sounds and ambience all come together to make a truly terrifying experience.  I don't want to give much away but this is the ONLY game I've ever had to stop playing because I was too chicken sh#t to continue.  I still can't stop playing it, I just play it in small doses now 😆 .  If you are a fan of horror games or just enjoy being scared as you figure out puzzles then this game is for you.  If you have a weak heart however, I'd look elsewhere.

Immersive and dreadful, the name of the game holds true to title. I just bought this game not knowing what to expect except the similar reviews you’re reading now, but man, nothing compares to being in the thick of this game. The audio and usage of light really makes the experience deep and almost tangible, this is definitely not one to be missed if you’re into dungeon crawlers and horror. This game is a roguelite and the layout of the map changes every session which gives it great replay-ability! Created by the same developer of the current game Cosmodread, the games are of similar fashion. 
I posted my session of Dreadhalls on YouTube yesterday if you’d like to have a quick peek in my channel VR RAW. I also have my Cosmodread session there as well.

I've been meaning to come back and stream this one on my Justini0 YouTube channel, but I gotta' be honest: I've never beaten it, because it's too scary! It doesn't look like it here, but when you're in there, the atmosphere makes it SUPER hard to not wet yourself... I've played through the Outlasts, through Phasmophobia in VR, through Dead Space, and many, many other horror games, and the only one that stacks next to this on a scary scale is Cosmodread. I've bought this game a second time on a family headset to have as a party game for guests, because it's just that great seeing people's reactions to it! I highly recommend you check it out, and I hope you can stay long enough to identify the different weaknesses of the enemies before they overcome you!
Get 25 p3rc3nt off my VR library at:
beacons DOT ai SLASH justinio
(Just change the CAPITAL WORDS, and scroll to the "Quest" drop-down menu at the bottom!)
Fun, scary, and unpredictable. Truly an amazing game.
This game is one of the few games that scares me enough to quit. That being said, it’s so fun I can’t stay away long. Hope for an update or even a sequel.
This game is so freaking scary definitely the scariest game I have played. If you love to be scared trust me this game will do the trick.
Hella scary but hella fun at the same time, plus very good price

This game Is old. Really old. It's one of the first vr titles ever made for oculus. Like Gen 1, no head tracking. It's come a long way since then, but that's because it's good. I've seen some reviews complaining that the level design seems randomly generated.  It is. That's literally the point. You don't know where the exit is. What's around the next corner? What was that noise? Limited resources, tight halls, and darkness waiting to swallow you whole. That's the game. There are some times when you'll barely see an enemy. Other times you'll be swarmed. It's built to be played over and over again! People claim they weren't scared. They're the type that pay to go to a haunted house just to say it wasn't scary. If you go in with no fear, it's nothing. If you let yourself get immersed and actually play the game, it's a blast.

I just bought this dreadhlls vr horror game. And when I first started it up. That main menu on dreadhalls was scary. And then I hit new game. All I have to say it felt so real the walls and the lantern. And then I started opening the first door in the small room. I started saying to myself nope. Right after I took a peek at the halls.
I like to play without using the lamp, it's so scary!

Pls run bruh 
gets tragic
It got me crying but I love it. Made me and my cousin scream very loud, it’s very scary.
really dumb this definitely should be in the free section. don’t waste your money. you’ve been warned. if this actually spooked out those reviewers they have issues

Sooo I bought this game as a first for this guy that I’m dating. While playing, I literally screamed and threw the headset 🤣 . The guy I’m dating literally did the same thing I did. Screamed bloody murder   very scary. Literally raised the hair on the back of my neck. Seriously love this game!!
it seemed a bit weird but bought and it was worth the price. the woman chasing u at some points was very scary but makes it a lot of fun

reviews.review_title: My aunt nearly had a panic attack
reviews.review_description:
She was about to be chased by the light hateing bat flesh pile thingy. Nearly had a panic attack
10/10 scary

an update is needed!!!
and I have a great and easy to implement idea! and also innovative and fun! Is there your email contact to describe this idea of ​​mine?
Definitely got my heart pumping

This was very first game I purchased years ago shortly after I purchased my oculus quest 2.  I was blown away by how sharp the graphics were!  I mean just look at your hands and look at the walls when you’re walking through… Excellent!   As the map changes this game never gets old; I am so glad I purchased this game.  Well worth it! ￼

The fact that you're bare bones, makes it amazing. Always looking around corners, woying about the lantern oil, the creepy wailing and whispering ambience, has made me a lifelong fan. Can't wait for the next one.❤️‍🔥
Very good horror game. A remake with better graphics would be amazing.

Surely you could update this for the quest 3 so it had the PC's dynamic shadows? Its.more then capable when you see other games that have highter graphic levels than this  Don't abandon this great game!
If you don't like jump scares don't get this!! Dark corridors, disturbing images, totally chilling with a good story line!
Horrible game, not interesting, not scary, please I need a refund ASAP!

reviews.review_title: Not as scary as I was expecting
reviews.review_description:
Every review I read for this game sounded like exactly what I was looking for. It was described as a difficult and challenging horror experience. I entered this game to find that it looks even more dated than the game it was obviously inspired by (Amnesia: The Dark Descent). Most of the scares were simple jump scares that made me gasp at most. And when a monster appeared it would either disappear really quickly or just melt into the floor. 

The game just seemed very one dimensional and simple and just underwhelming in terms of immersion. Is there something i’m missing? Did I not get far enough in? My experience did not reflect the reviews that I read.

reviews.review_title: Scariest f ing game I've ever played.
reviews.review_description: Words cannot even describe the feelings you get when you play this game. The atmosphere/environment, the Audio, the randomness from the randomly generated maps so you don't know when any will be around the next corner, everything is absolutely 100% creepy and you feel like you are trapped in this place. I cannot finish this game. Every time I try, this game makes me fear for my life, literally. Absolutely a must have game. It's that immersive and hands down the number 1 horror game out. Don't believe me? Try it for yourself, and then get back to me.

First official VR purchase. 
Not disappointed one bit. 
Interesting story. 
Creepy atmosphere. 
Suspenseful af. 
Reminiscent of Outlast with a dash of Resident Evil. 
If you love to be incredibly spooked , you need this game!!
My husband 13yr old stepson and I have fun with this game. We've all had jump scare screams! I chicken out a bit on it. Freaks me out. Lol.

reviews.review_title: Unable to play due to motion sickness
reviews.review_description: The game is atmospheric, but unplayable for me due to there being no way of moving without motion sickness.  If the game is updated to allow jump moves, as is standard in other games, I'll update my review.

All the ingredients are there the minute you launch the game.Creepy music, scary sounds, darkness and the creatures you bump into and the game gets a lot more unsettling once you have encountered your first creature.That happened for me after seeing the dog face to face as it lunged at 
me straight out of the darkness and killed me.From that point on every corner makes you wonder if something is waiting for you the minute you turn.

If you compare this game to Exorcist, for me it delivers the horror in a much better way as it is constant.The most comfortable you will ever be in this game is uneasy and unsettled which changes to fear when you bump into the dungeon’s residents, whereas apart from the horror sequences in the Exorcist, the game isn’t scary at all.

This is also a great app for introducing a horror game to friends who are new to VR as you just launch and go rather than them having to solve a heap of puzzles before getting to any horror type content.

At 9.99€ this really is a no brainer.Get the game and start getting scared.

Just a tiny suggestion to the devs though.Ask the player whether they are male or female and have the corresponding sounds based on their choice.The immersion was broken a bit for my wife when she tried the game as the sounds of a man panting rather than a woman was naturally unrealistic for her.
I’ve always loved horror games, and this one gave me jump scares so bad that I had to take a break. I even got a little nauseous. What a rush.

I got the oculus for my son for Christmas. Little did I know that I’d be quickly swept into the VR horror world! This game made me jump many times in the beginning and I am not faint of heart. I love hoodie movies and haunted houses! I went back to it night after night until I finished it. I enjoyed it very much. The only recommendation I would make was that the halls and maps got kind of repetitive. But overall worth the money!
Nerve racking. My fiancé won’t even play it. By far one of the best games on here

This is honestly one of my favorite horror games. I remember years ago watching Markiplier the YouTuber play it. Ever since that I've wanted to try it out myself. I am very impressed by the atmosphere, monsters, and the gameplay. This game genuinely gives you a feeling of horror and dread. I played it alone at night with Sony sound cancelling headphones and I must say I legitimately had a feeling of being in danger. Expecially using my own body to turn around physically instead of using the controller. Peaking around corners is so real feeling. This game is a must for just $10.

reviews.review_title: scarier than I thought it would be
reviews.review_description: this game scared the crap out of me. I don't know if I can finish it.... I am recommending it tho if ur into creepy rooms and dark hallways
I am a horror movie fan, but I can’t play this game. For a few attempts, I could just walk to the corner and I don’t think I can move any further. Help me!
Seriously scary, simple satisfying gameplay, the 3D VR is really good
I've literally screamed several times- which is a win in my book.

Seriously, don't. I have played all the horror you can think of, nothing scared me like this, especially in VR. Before I played I was like yeah no problem it can't be that scary right? Wrong! The dark halls, the jumpscares, the atmosphere, everything comes together perfectly, and on top of that it never gets boring because the dungeons are procedurally generated. Definitely worth the money.
My voice is gone from screaming.. it’s AWESOME!
10/10 spooky. Scared my sockskskksks off.
